last_item: store/auth InitAuth reset-password branch (internal/store/auth.go:44) — the docstring claims InitAuth 'overwrites any existing row (used by install and reset-password)' with ON CONFLICT resetting failed_attempts=0/locked_until=NULL, but TestAuthRoundTrip only ever hits the INSERT (calls InitAuth once); the reset UPDATE branch + its security-critical session-secret rotation was unproven at the store level, and the live caller SetPassword routes existing accounts to SetPasswordHash so nothing else exercises it either (band-1 correctness)
last_result: SHIPPED (test only, no bug): added TestInitAuthOverwritesExistingRowAndResetsLockout to internal/store/store_test.go. Drives the untested ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE branch — init, SetLockout(5,+1h), re-init with new hash+secret — and asserts the reset (1) overwrites the password hash, (2) ROTATES the session secret (so tokens minted under the old secret can't be replayed after a reset — the security point of a reset), (3) clears failed_attempts+locked_until, (4) bumps updated_at, (5) updates the single row in place (COUNT(*)=1). No bug: the ON CONFLICT clause is correct; the test locks the previously-unproven documented contract. Mutation-proven (perl in-place apply + git checkout revert, safe since auth.go is unmodified): keep-old-hash / drop-secret-rotation / keep-old-failed_attempts / keep-old-locked_until / DO-NOTHING / updated_at=auth.updated_at(keep-stale) each red the test; baseline green each time. code-reviewer clean. test-skeptic found ONE escaping mutation on the first draft (stale updated_at — the test didn't assert on UpdatedAt) -> added a strict-After assertion (flake-safe: a full GetAuth round-trip + asserts run between the two writes so the wall clock advances) -> re-verified the mutation is now caught. Gates green (gofmt clean, go vet exit 0, go test ./... exit 0, CGO_ENABLED=0 static build); web untouched; e2e not needed (pure unit).
notes: Never-ending hardening loop. Bias: (A) prove vibe-coded code actually does what it claims with tests that fail if behavior breaks; (P) preflight every risky/destructive/external op and fail loud+actionable BEFORE acting; (D) tighten defaults + self-heal so a bad config can't wedge Postgres. Re-seed the backlog via a parallel audit panel (Mode S) when it runs thin — this loop does NOT self-complete. Shared defaults live in scripts/ralph/DEFAULTS.md; real sm behavior at /primary01/git/server-management/src/sm/. Run go/make outside the sandbox if snap-confine blocks it.
north_star: An indie hacker runs a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI on best defaults — never loses data, never gets wedged, never confused. Every risky path is preflighted, every failure is loud and actionable, every claim is covered by a test that would fail if the behavior broke.
